original: Gamma ray burst (GRB) 090423 is observed for 10 seconds, the most distant object of any kind and also the oldest known object in the universe
Ray Tomlinson, American computer programmer (invented email and the @ sign), born in Amsterdam, New York (d. 2016)
YouTube's co-founder Jawed Karim uploads the first video to YouTube, "Me at the zoo" showing him in front of two elephants at the San Diego Zoo. Has over 311 million views.
Apollo 16 astronauts explore the moon's surface
World's first malaria vaccine, giving partial protection to children, begins in Malawi by the WHO
McDonald's opens its 1st restaurant in China, amassing over 40,000 customers on its opening day
New Coke debuts; Coca-Cola announced it is changing its secret flavor formula
Stuart Orkin, American stem cell biologist whose research led to first gene therapy using CRISPR to treat sickle cell disease, born in New York City[1]
First findings of a frog fossil 40 million years old, found on Seymour Island, Antarctica (2015) published in "Scientific Reports"
Max Planck, German physicist, father of Quantum Physics (Planck Constant, Nobel 1918), born in Kiel, Duchy of Holstein (d. 1947)
Greek Olympic cyclist Kanellos Kanellopoulos pedals the MIT Daedalus 88 self-powered aircraft a record 71.5 miles (115 km) from Heraklion on the island of Crete to Santorini
The 8-bit personal home computer the Sinclair ZX Spectrum is released (goes on to sell 5 million worldwide)
Conch Republic is established - secession of the Florida Keys from the United States of America
King Brian Boru of Ireland defeats Viking forces at the Battle of Clontarf, freeing Ireland from foreign control
Granville Woods, American inventor, 1st post civil war African American mechanical and electrical engineer, invented telegraph for railways, born in Columbus, Ohio (d. 1910)[1]
Chuck Feeney, American businessman (Duty Free Shoppers Group) and philanthropist (The Atlantic Philanthropies) who gave away almost his entire $8B fortune, born in Elizabeth, New Jersey (d. 2023)
Karolina Szabó runs female world record for 25k (1:29:29.2) and 30k (1:47:05.6) in Budapest, Hungary
West Indian cricketerChris Gaylesmashes the fastest century in history in just 30 balls
Fatboy Slimreleases single "Weapon of Choice", music video directed by Spike Jonze starringChristopher Walkendancing
Details of ancient Greek philosopherPlato's last night and burial place revealed by deciphering a carbonized scroll from Herculaneum, using new technologies including AI
Beijing closes all schools for two weeks because of the SARS virus
International Coral Reef Initiative releases study showing that 84% of the world's coral reefs have been impacted by an ongoing bleaching event that started in January 2023
Disney World breaks ground for Victorian themed Grand Floridian Beach Resort (now known as Disney's Grand Floridian Resort & Spa)
Hervé Villechaize, French-American actor (Fantasy Island - Ze plane! Ze plane!), born in Paris (d. 1993)
Steve McCurry, American photographer (Afghan Girl), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Marvel's "Avengers: Infinity War" directed by Anthony and Joe Russo, starringChris Evans,Robert Downey Jrand large ensemble cast premieres in Los Angeles, California
American Simone Leigh is the first Black woman artist to win the main prize at Venice Biennale for her sculpture "Brick House"
Lee Miller, American photographer (War correspondent for Vogue in WWII), born in Poughkeepsie, New York (d. 1977)
Southampton striker Shane Long scores fastest goal in English Premier League history when he nets after 7.69 seconds in 1-1 draw at Watford
MLB Boston Red Sox outfielder Masataka Yoshida hits a solo home run and a grand slam in the eight inning in 12-5 win over the Brewers in Milwaukee
NFL Draft: LSU quarterbackJoe Burrowfirst pick by Cincinnati Bengals
NFL Draft: #1 pick UCLA quarterback Troy Aikman by Dallas Cowboys
Ted Williamshits his 1st Major League home run while playing for the Boston Red Sox
Tony Esposito, Canadian Hockey Hall of Fame goaltender (6 x NHL All Star; Vezina Trophy 1970, 72, 74; Chicago Blackhawks), born in Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario (d. 2021)
Stanley Cup Final, Olympia Stadium, Detroit, MI: Pete Babando scores Cup-winning goal in double overtime of Game 7 as Detroit Red Wings beat NY Rangers, 4-3 for a 4-3 series win
Charley Paddock runs a world record 100m in 10.4 seconds
First decimal coins issued in Britain, the 5 and 10 new pence, replacing the shilling and two-shilling pieces

Duke Wilhelm IV of Bavaria endorses "The German Beer Purity Law" (Reinheitsgebot) and adds to it standards for the sale of beer in Bavaria, ensuring beer is only brewed from three ingredients: water, barley, and hops
Oldest US public institution, Boston Latin School founded
President-electGeorge Washingtonmoves into Franklin House, New York, first official residence of a US President
Molniya-1, 1st Soviet communications satellite launches
Ranger 4, 1st US satellite to reach the Moon, launched from Cape Canaveral
Howard Engstrom, American computer designer (co-creator Univac computer), born in Boston, Massachusetts (d. 1962)
Charles Warrell, English schoolteacher and creator of the I-Spy books, born in Farmborough, Somerset (d. 1995)
Thomas Potter Cooke, English actor, first person to portray Frankenstein on stage, born in London (d. 1864)[1]
William Shakespeare's "The Merry Wives of Windsor" is first performed withQueen Elizabeth Iof England in attendance
Vitascope system of movie projection 1st shown at Koster & Bial's Music Hall, New York City
"I Can't Help Myself (Sugar Pie Honey Bunch)" single released by the Four Tops (Billboard Song of the Year 1965)
Kawhi Leonardis named the NBA Defensive Player of the Year for the first time
MLB Chicago Federals host the Kansas City Packers in the 1st game played at Weeghman Park (now Wrigley Field)
Chevron Championship Women's Golf, The Club at Carlton Woods: Lilia Vu wins her first major beating fellow American Angel Yin on the first playoff hole
Brooklyn College soccer team wins Nepal's All-Nepal Football Association invitational tournament 2-0
ADO Den Haag soccer team forms in The Hague, Netherlands
Second of two major earthquakes strikes island of Samar, Philippines, magnitude 6.3, a day after magnitude 6.1 hits Luzon Island killing at least 16 people
Over 1000 square miles flooded in Shantung Province, China
153-year old De Adriaan Windmill in Haarlem, the Netherlands, burns down
English steamship "Great Western" crosses the Atlantic docks in NYC
Irish mathematician and astronomer William Rowan Hamilton presents his Theory of Systems of Rays
Turkish Grand National Assembly 1st meets in Ankara, under the leadership ofMustafa Kemal Atatürkdenounces the government of Sultan Mehmed VI and announces a temporary constitution
Queen Anneis crowned at Westminster Abbey, London
